rationale: The evidence pattern is consistent with Impacket's wmiexec.py lateral movement tool: WmiPrvSE.exe spawning cmd.exe with output redirected to ADMIN$ share temp files (EV-001, A0, EV-003) is the hallmark of WMI-based remote command execution. EV-004 and EV-005 show a second execution method — services.exe writing a randomly-named batch file (rIIBPrqF.bat) to C:\Windows\ and executing it via cmd.exe at SYSTEM integrity, consistent with Impacket's smbexec.py technique, which creates a temporary service to run attacker-controlled commands. The combination of two distinct remote execution frameworks (WMI and SMB service abuse), use of ADMIN$ and C$ shares for I/O redirection, and escalation to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM (EV-005, EV-006) confirms active lateral movement or remote code execution against this host. The execution of calc.exe (EV-002) under these conditions is likely a proof-of-concept or capability test by the attacker.
